one_line_takeaway: By retargeting jailbreak optimization from an exact affirmative token string to an entire class of semantically-equivalent harmful responses under a perplexity-coherence constraint, SRA/SRA+ achieves near-perfect (99.71% average, often 100%) attack success across 26 open-source LLMs with far shorter, lower-perplexity, defense-resistant prompts than GCG/AutoDAN/BEAST, and is the first attack in this comparison to break GPT-5 at all (18.67% vs 0.00% for every baseline).
key_finding: On HarmBench across 26 open-source LLMs (Table II), SRA+ achieves a 99.71% average ASR, reaching 100% ASR on 24 of 26 models, versus the best baseline averages of 63.80% (GCG), 59.20% (AutoDAN), and 59.03% (TAP); plain SRA (without the proxy) averages 84.43% and specifically underperforms on narrow-distribution model families (e.g., 17.33% on Llama 2 70B, 19.00% on Llama 2 13B, 30.33% on Llama 2 7B), a gap SRA+ closes to 100% on all three. Efficiency/naturalness/robustness on AdvBench (Table III, following BEAST's protocol) against Vicuna 7B/13B, Mistral 7B, Guanaco 7B: within a 15-second compute budget SRA achieves 95.77% (Vicuna 7B), 86.73% (Vicuna 13B), 100.0% (Mistral 7B) and 100.0% (Guanaco 7B) ASR with PPL scores of 24.21/25.43/36.75/26.05 -- an average perplexity reduction of 96.8% versus GCG, 60.0% versus AutoDAN, and 70.6% versus BEAST at matched budgets. Under PPL-based defense at intensity 5 (Table III ASRD), SRA retains 95.96%/85.19%/99.62%/99.62% ASR versus GCG collapsing to 0.96-31.54%. Against SmoothLLM (Table VII, 100 samples), SRA maintains 92-100% ASR across Defenseless/Swap/Insert/Patch settings versus PAIR (48-76%) and AutoDAN (56-90%). Transferability (Table VIII): attacks generated on Vicuna 7B and transferred to Vicuna 13B reach 84.42% ASR (15s budget) for SRA versus 28.46% (AutoDAN) and 15.77% (BEAST); Guanaco 7B to Vicuna 13B reaches 79.42% versus 3.46% for BEAST. Against closed-source commercial models (Table IX): SRA+ achieves 46.67% ASR on GPT-4.1 (best baseline TAP: 43.33%) and 18.67% ASR on GPT-5, where every other baseline attack scores exactly 0.00%. A granularity ablation (Table X) shows GPT-5 ASR rising from 1.67% (1-token expansion) to a peak of 18.67% (4-token expansion) then falling to 16.00% (8-token), motivating the multi-token-chunk SRHS variant. Successful adversarial suffixes are extremely short (Table XI: mean 1.34-2.61 tokens across models) compared to GCG (~20 tokens) or AutoDAN (60+ tokens).
core_contribution: The paper's own contribution is the Semantic Representation Attack (SRA), an LLM-agnostic jailbreak paradigm that reformulates the token-level adversarial objective (forcing a fixed affirmative string like 'Sure, here is...') into a semantic-representation objective: find an adversarial suffix x* that maximizes probability mass over an entire equivalence class Y_Phi of responses sharing the same malicious meaning Phi, subject to a perplexity (coherence) constraint. The authors prove two original theorems: the 'Coherence-Convergence Relationship' (Theorem III.1, bounding a prompt's perplexity guarantees convergence to semantically equivalent responses) and a 'Cross-Model Semantic Generalization' bound (Theorem III.2, bounding the probability divergence of inducing the same semantic representation Phi across a source and target model as a function of the perplexity threshold and inter-model distance). They operationalize these as the Semantic Representation Heuristic Search (SRHS) algorithm, a heuristic tree search that expands an adversarial prompt token-by-token (or in k-token chunks) only along branches satisfying sufficient perplexity-coherence conditions (Theorem III.4), reducing worst-case search complexity from exponential O(V^N) to O(N*min(tau^l, eta)). A second instantiation, SRA+, anchors the search on a fixed 'semantic proxy' model (Vicuna 7B) rather than the target model itself, specifically to counteract probability suppression in models with narrow/peaked output distributions (e.g., the Llama 2 family). This journal version extends an earlier NeurIPS 2025 conference paper (ref [33]) by adding the Cross-Model Semantic Generalization theorem, the multi-token-chunk generalization of SRHS, the SRA+ proxy mechanism, and a substantially larger experimental suite (closed-source GPT-4.1/GPT-5 transfer, SmoothLLM defense, granularity ablation, prompt-length analysis).
threat_model: White-box and black-box adversary attacking aligned (safety-tuned) LLMs via automated prompt-suffix optimization. White-box SRA/SRA+ assume token-probability/logit access to the target (or a semantic proxy) model to run the heuristic search; the resulting adversarial suffixes are then evaluated both in-model (white-box) and transferred zero-shot to other models, including closed-source commercial APIs (GPT-4.1, GPT-5) accessed only as black boxes. The attacker's goal is jailbreaking: appending a short, semantically coherent, low-perplexity suffix to a harmful user query so that the target LLM produces a harmful response that is semantically equivalent to (not verbatim identical to) a canonical affirmative response, thereby evading both alignment training and downstream defenses such as perplexity filters, SmoothLLM-style perturbation defenses, and (partially) commercial content-moderation layers.
limitations: The paper's own stated limitation (Conclusion section): SRA/SRA+ rely on open-source models as white-box or proxy anchors for black-box attacks, and remain limited by independent, post-generation content-filter layers on commercial APIs (e.g., Azure OpenAI content filters) -- the authors observe cases where the underlying model begins generating harmful content but an external moderation layer intercepts and blocks the final output, explaining why GPT-5 ASR (18.67%) remains far below open-source ASR (~100%) despite the underlying jailbreak succeeding at the model level. Additional caveats evident from the paper's own text: the R(y)=Phi harmfulness judge (a fine-tuned Llama-2-13B classifier plus keyword filter) is itself an approximate, learned oracle rather than ground truth, so ASR numbers inherit that judge's error profile; several 2026 closed-weight comparison baselines (GCG-M/T, PEZ, GBDA, UAT, AP, SFS, PAP-top5 in Table IX) could not be run against GPT-4.1/GPT-5 at all because they require white-box gradient access, limiting the black-box baseline comparison largely to PAIR/TAP/AutoDAN/HJ/DR; and larger models (DeepSeek R1 70B, Qwen 72B, Mistral 8x7B) are missing entries for several older baselines in Table II (marked '-'), so the 'average' baseline ASRs are computed over partially incomplete rows for those methods.

datasets_used: HarmBench (standard and contextual behaviors, used for effectiveness evaluation)
AdvBench (used for efficiency/naturalness/robustness/transferability comparisons following BEAST's protocol)
quantitative_results: SRA+ average ASR = 99.71% across 26 open-source LLMs (Table II)
SRA+ = 100% ASR on 24 of 26 evaluated open-source models
SRA (no proxy) average ASR = 84.43%; underperforms on Llama 2 70B (17.33%), Llama 2 13B (19.00%), Llama 2 7B (30.33%)

Baseline averages on Table II: GCG 63.80%, AutoDAN 59.20%, TAP 59.03%, PAIR 54.26%, GCG-T 52.90%, GCG-M 51.48%, AP 49.87%, UAT 36.34%, GBDA 35.52%, ZS 35.38%, DR (Direct Request) 35.66%, SFS 43.54%, PEZ 34.78%, PAP-top5 23.22%, HJ 40.51%

15s-budget AdvBench ASR (Table III): SRA 95.77% (Vicuna 7B) / 86.73% (Vicuna 13B) / 100.0% (Mistral 7B) / 100.0% (Guanaco 7B), vs BEAST 77.12%/37.69%/42.12%/99.62% and AutoDAN 75.19%/39.27%/97.31%/99.81%
15s-budget PPL of SRA prompts: 24.21 (Vicuna 7B), 25.43 (Vicuna 13B), 36.75 (Mistral 7B), 26.05 (Guanaco 7B); average PPL reduction of 96.8% vs GCG, 60.0% vs AutoDAN, 70.6% vs BEAST
ASR under PPL defense intensity 5 (ASRD, Table III): SRA 95.96%/85.19%/99.62%/99.62% at 15s vs GCG 0.96-31.54%
SmoothLLM defense ASR (100 samples, Table VII): SRA 92% (defenseless) to 100% (Swap/Patch) / 96% (Insert), vs PAIR 76%->48-62% and AutoDAN 90%->56-78%
Transfer ASR Vicuna 7B -> Vicuna 13B at 15s (Table VIII): SRA 84.42% vs AutoDAN 28.46% vs BEAST 15.77% vs GCG 2.88%
Transfer ASR Guanaco 7B -> Vicuna 13B at 15s: SRA 79.42% vs BEAST 3.46%

Closed-source transfer (Table IX): GPT-4.1 ASR = 46.67% (SRA+), 44.33% (SRA), 43.33% (TAP, best baseline); GPT-5 ASR = 18.67% (SRA+), 1.67% (SRA), 0.00% for every other listed baseline (PAIR, TAP, AutoDAN, HJ, DR, GCG-T)
GPT-5 granularity ablation (Table X): ASR = 1.67% (1-token chunks), 16.67% (2-token), 18.67% (4-token), 16.00% (8-token)
Average successful adversarial suffix length (Table XI): 1.34 tokens (Starling 7B) to 2.61 tokens (Vicuna 13B), vs ~20 tokens for GCG and 60+ for AutoDAN

Coherence threshold ablation on Vicuna 13B (Table IV): ASR at 240s ranges from 96.15% (tau=10 or 20) down to 96.15% (tau=1000), with tau=20 chosen as the fair-comparison default because tau<20 occasionally collapses the valid token pool
baselines_compared: AutoDAN (both Liu et al. and Zhu et al. variants)
SmoothLLM (as a defense evaluated against, not an attack baseline)
